exercises.title: Unit Tests with Assert
exercises.description: Write unit tests using Python's built-in `assert` statement (simulating pytest). Test a `SalesProcessor` class that computes revenue, filters records, and generates summaries. Write at least 8 test cases covering normal cases, edge cases, and error cases.
exercises.expectedOutput:
PASS: test_total_revenue
PASS: test_empty_total
PASS: test_filter_category
PASS: test_filter_empty_category
PASS: test_top_n
PASS: test_average_price
PASS: test_average_price_empty
PASS: test_category_summary
hints: The SalesProcessor is already implemented — all tests should pass if you just run them.
run_test() calls the function and catches AssertionError.
Category summary for widgets: 2 records, revenue = 10*5 + 20*3 = 50 + 60 = 110.

exercises.title: Mock External Dependencies
exercises.description: Test automation code that has external dependencies (HTTP API, email) by replacing them with mocks. Write a `mock_api_call(url)` function that returns fake data, and test a `DataFetcher` class that normally calls an API but during tests uses the mock.
exercises.expectedOutput:
PASS: test_fetch_users
PASS: test_fetch_user
PASS: test_fetch_missing_user
PASS: test_fetch_stats
PASS: test_call_log
hints: In make_mock_client, the inner function maps URLs to responses.
if '/api/users/' in url: return {'user': None}  # Catches /api/users/99

warmup.title: Quick Recall: assert statement
